title: Museum Gallery Tour: Recovering the Visitor-Flow Causal Map
isolation: candidate runs as an isolated stdin->stdout subprocess via isorun; the ground-truth DAG, topological order, noisy-OR weights, leak rates and hidden state stay in the evaluator process
strategies: predict the empty visitor-flow map (SHD = number of true edges = the evaluator baseline -> ~0.1 everywhere)
marginal mutual-information thresholding with index-order orientation: catches real driver-galleries but drowns in transitive-path false positives and coin-flip orientations

conditional-independence (PC-style) skeleton -- marginal-MI edges pruned when a third gallery screens them off (MI(X,Y|Z)~0) -- oriented by ascending marginal entry frequency (rarer gallery = source): removes transitive false edges and recovers most orientations, but finite tours and near-saturated descendants leave residual errors
score- or constraint-based discrete BN discovery (larger conditioning sets, BDeu/BIC search, or an explicit noisy-OR likelihood) that models each museum's structure to push SHD lower still
